key_strengths: QTL patent portfolio (140,000+ patents, #2 globally in 5G SEPs) is a structural toll-road on global wireless with ~74% operating margins
Exceptional cash generation: $12.8B FCF on $44.3B revenue (29% FCF margin) with fabless model keeping capex at only 2.7% of revenue
Strong balance sheet: A/A2-rated, 18.6x interest coverage, 1.06x debt/EBITDA, $13.8B in cash
Credible diversification into automotive and IoT (combined 27% YoY growth in FY2025, targeting $22B by FY2029)
Stock down 34% from 52-week high; trading at 12.9x forward earnings and 8.6% FCF yield vs 13-year median EV/EBITDA of 12.8x
key_risks: Apple modem exit confirmed — zero Apple chip supply by FY2027, representing $5–7B annual QCT revenue impact
China concentration at 46% of total revenue — highest among major U.S. semiconductor companies — is a binary risk from trade escalation or 'buy local' mandates
QTL licensing durability at risk from FRAND court challenges or 6G SEP competition from Huawei; Huawei licensing renewal unconfirmed as of March 2026
Diversification execution risk — bull case requires automotive ($8B) and IoT ($14B) targets by FY2029, which are plausible but not yet proven
ARM/RISC-V architecture disruption risk over a 5–10 year horizon; Qualcomm won ARM lawsuit in January 2026 but long-term risk persists
red_flags: CEO received a 15% pay raise in a year when GAAP net income fell 45%
Insider ownership is low — management personal wealth not materially at risk alongside shareholders
China at 46% of revenue is the highest concentration among major U.S. semiconductor peers
Apple chip exit is confirmed (not forecast) — $5–7B annual revenue loss by FY2027 with no certainty that diversification absorbs it in time
No confirmed renewal of Huawei licensing agreement; QTL growth guidance is zero for the near term
52-week range of $120.80–$205.95 shows extreme volatility; beta of 1.57 amplifies tail risk from China or licensing events
buy_triggers: Stock falls to $105–$115 (approximately 10–12x bear-case owner earnings of ~$9B), creating a genuine margin of safety
Apple modem transition fully absorbed in reported earnings (FY2027+) and automotive achieves $4B+ annual revenue run rate
Huawei licensing renewal confirmed, adding $1B+ annually back to QTL and signaling China relationship stabilization
sell_triggers: China revenue declines more than 25% over any rolling 12-month period without clear offsetting growth elsewhere
QTL faces a successful legal or regulatory challenge that materially reduces its patent royalty rates
Automotive revenue misses the $4B annual target by end of FY2026 with no credible revised timeline from management
valuation_summary: At ~$136, QCOM trades at 12.9x forward earnings, 8.6% FCF yield, and 10.7x EV/EBITDA versus a 13-year median of 12.8x — appearing modestly undervalued in a base case (DCF present value ~$130–$149B) but fairly valued given wide outcome dispersion from Apple exit and China risk. A genuine margin of safety requires a price of $105–$115; downside in the bear case is 30–35%.
source_summary: Analysis based on Qualcomm Q4 FY2025 and Q1 FY2026 earnings releases, FY2025 10-K SEC filings, S&P and Moody's credit ratings, patent analytics sources (LexisNexis, GreyB, Lumenci), and web searches from March 2026, supplemented by training knowledge for business model context.
